text:
Market today: Inflation fears are back with surging oil and rising 10-year Treasury yields, creating headwinds for your concentrated tech/chip book. JPMorgan is also warning about AI stocks echoing a 90s split.

Your holdings: SOXL (-36.75%) and SPAL (-57.73%) are your biggest concerns; both are leveraged ETFs, decaying, and SPAL has blown past its stop. QQQ (-2.73%) and QQQI (-5.03%) also need trimming. Margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on your chip names and tech ETFs for any signs of weakness. Watch for continued spikes in yields and oil.

Bottom line: High inflation and AI warnings mean it's time to play defense and manage risk.
plays: SOXL: Consider trimming or cutting losses given the -36.75% and 'SELL' status. Respect the original stop at 200, or set a new one tighter.
SPAL: This is already well past its stop of 21.38 at 10.63. Cut losses immediately.
QQQ/QQQI: Trim these positions as indicated by the scanner, especially with rising yield/inflation concerns.
